issues: 1x personality loss — face details vanish at native size, only boxy silhouette survives
dark-bg contrast weak on lower-body brown tones — merges with near-black surround
charm is retro-pixel not soft/round — passes sticker test but leans 'blocky toy' over 'friendly soft toy'
reasoning: Pixel-art box with warm orange/yellow/brown palette and blue-teal top cap. Silhouette reads unambiguously as a box/chest at all scales — strong shape language. Warm colors and implied face give genuine personality at 4x; the design has sticker-test coherence. On light bg contrast is solid. On dark bg the mid-brown body walls blend into the surround, reducing edge crispness. At 1x the face cues (eyes, expression) disappear entirely — only a boxy blob remains, which is readable but charmless at that scale. Overall: charm is present and distinct, silhouette is clean, but dark-bg edge bleed and 1x personality loss keep this below the 8-pass threshold.
